title: Homalium racoubea
parts: I couldn't find any information regarding Homalium racoubea's medicinal parts.
preparations: Homalium racoubea is reported to be used in herbal preparations. In traditional medicine, its bark is used for various purposes. The plant's extracts are said to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Some studies suggest that it may have potential in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. However, further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety in herbal preparations.
intro: Homalium racoubea, also known as "Homalium", is a medicinal plant species within the family Salicaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Homalium racoubea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Homalium racoubea is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, particularly in West African and Southeast Asian cultures.
